headline: UK wholesale at the higher end of its recent range
commentary: UK power has spent 2026 swinging between roughly £70 and £110/MWh, and currently sits near the top of that band. Gas — which sets the UK power price the majority of the time — is back at a four-week high after bottoming in late June, so the market is climbing again. Over five years wholesale power has ranged from about £55 to £127/MWh, and that band stays wide year on year: the point of a fixed contract isn't that today's price is unusually cheap, it's that it converts a moving, unpredictable cost into a flat line you can budget around for the term.

gas.note: NBP gas futures at a four-week high, up from a ~99 p/therm low on 25 Jun.
